edgeCases: centinela start with workflow.use_worktrees=true provisions .worktrees/<feature>/ with a matching branch and per-worktree .workflow/ state
centinela start with workflow.use_worktrees=false runs the legacy single-checkout flow and never creates .worktrees/
wizard and centinela migrate both write .worktrees/ into .gitignore, .eslintignore, .prettierignore, .dockerignore, .rgignore, and tsconfig.json exclude, idempotently
centinela start on a feature whose .worktrees/<feature>/ already exists resumes in place: existing branch and .workflow/ state are preserved, no error raised
centinela merge with a dirty main working tree fails fast before git merge runs, leaves the worktree untouched, and prints a commit-or-stash instruction

centinela merge runs a spec-conflict pre-check across every active worktree by reading specs/*.feature and blocks the merge when two scenarios assert different observable outcomes for the same Given context (names both files and the scenario)
centinela merge on a clean text merge whose post-merge centinela validate passes does not invoke the Steward and removes .worktrees/<feature>/ after success
centinela merge invokes the Merge Steward whenever git reports a text conflict, passing the conflicted paths and the feature spec
centinela merge invokes the Merge Steward whenever the post-merge centinela validate fails (semantic conflict), passing the failing validate output and the feature spec

Merge Steward must escalate to the user on any non-high-confidence resolution: exit without modifying main, surface the proposed diff and reason, and record both in .workflow/<feature>-merge-steward.json
Worktree removal only happens after a fully successful merge (clean merge + passing validate, or Steward-applied high-confidence fix); any failure or escalation leaves the worktree intact
